titleEn: “We are expanding our apple range with the Strauss EW20 multi-head weigher”
titleZh: Maf Roda Agrobotic推出Strauss EW20多头秤，补齐苹果重量包装短板
summaryZh: Maf Roda Agrobotic通过收购德国Strauss公司，引入EW20多头秤，填补了其在苹果重量包装领域的产品空白。该设备采用关联称重技术，能自动匹配最佳水果组合以达到目标重量，减少浪费和水果处理。它支持袋装和托盘包装，灵活适应不同出口市场需求，尤其在德国已广泛应用。文章展示了自动化包装技术在果蔬采后处理中的创新应用，对智慧农业从业者具有参考价值。
featuredReason: 该新闻展示了自动化称重包装技术在果蔬采后处理中的最新应用，对智慧农业从业者了解如何通过精准包装减少浪费、拓展出口市场具有实践参考价值。
contentFull: Jonathan Chiavassa, Maf Roda Agrobotic: “We are expanding our apple range with the Strauss EW20 multi-head weigher” With the acquisition of the German company Strauss Verpackungsmaschinen, announced in early 2025, Maf Roda Agrobotic is continuing to integrate new technologies into its portfolio of solutions. Among these innovations, the EW20 multi-head weigher strengthens the group's offering in the apple packaging segment, an area that had previously been missing from its range. An equipment to complete the apple offeringAs a global leader in fruit and vegetable sorting, grading, and packaging systems, Maf Roda Agrobotic already provides solutions covering the entire post-harvest process. The integration of Strauss technology now allows the group to further expand its expertise in weight-based packaging solutions. "Until now, we did not have a multi-head weigher specifically dedicated to apples. This machine genuinely completes our range," explains Jonathan Chiavassa, Sales Director for France, Northern Spain, and Portugal at Maf Roda Agrobotic. "We already had several multi-head weigher installations for tomatoes, but nothing designed for automated apple packaging. This solution, therefore, represents a real added value within our packaging offering." Designed for automated packaging into bags or trays, the EW20 uses associative weighing technology to automatically identify the optimal combination of fruit to reach the target weight with a high level of accuracy. This approach reduces giveaway while also limiting fruit handling. © Maf-Roda Agrobotic Adapting to changing packaging market trendsThe introduction of this new solution also enables Maf Roda Agrobotic to address market segments where packaging is based primarily on weight rather than on fruit size. "This machine allows us to target markets where products are packed according to a defined weight rather than a calibrated size," explains Christophe Nivet. "It offers a high degree of flexibility, as a single system can be configured with one outlet connected to a bagging machine and another to a tray-packing line." This versatility is particularly attractive for operators active in export markets, where presentation requirements vary depending on the destination. "Certain markets, particularly the United States (outside Europe), the United Kingdom, and even Germany within Europe, have a strong preference for bagged apples, a format that remains less developed in some other European countries. Thanks to this machine, our customers can broaden their product offering and access new markets. For exporters, this is a significant advantage." © Maf-Roda Agrobotic A technology already well established in GermanyAlthough the EW20 remains relatively new to the French market, it already benefits from a strong reputation across the Rhine. Developed by Strauss, a company based near Hamburg, the machine is widely used in German fruit packing facilities. "It is a very common piece of equipment in Germany and has an excellent reputation. In France, it is still relatively unknown, which gives us a real opportunity for development," says Jonathan Chiavassa. © Maf-Roda Agrobotic An acquisition creating new opportunitiesBeyond the EW20 itself, the integration of Strauss technologies is part of a broader strategy aimed at strengthening Maf Roda Agrobotic's complete range of solutions. The objective is to provide growers and packers with increasingly comprehensive systems, from fruit reception through to final packaging. "The integration of Strauss technologies into our portfolio fully reflects our ambition to provide producers and packers with ever more innovative and efficient solutions," says Fabrice Blanc, Managing Director of Maf Roda Agrobotic. With this new associative weighing solution, the group now aims to support operators looking to diversify their packaging formats and expand into new markets, particularly internationally. translationZh:
标题：“我们通过Strauss EW20多头秤扩展苹果产品线”
内容：Jonathan Chiavassa，Maf Roda Agrobotic：“我们通过Strauss EW20多头秤扩展苹果产品线”
随着2025年初宣布收购德国公司Strauss Verpackungsmaschinen，Maf Roda Agrobotic正在持续将新技术整合到其解决方案组合中。在这些创新中，EW20多头秤增强了集团在苹果包装领域的产品供应，而这一领域此前在其产品线中是缺失的。

一款完善苹果产品线的设备
作为果蔬分选、分级和包装系统的全球领导者，Maf Roda Agrobotic已经提供覆盖采后全流程的解决方案。Strauss技术的整合使集团能够进一步扩展其在基于重量的包装解决方案方面的专业知识。“在此之前，我们没有专门用于苹果的多头秤。这台机器真正完善了我们的产品线，”Maf Roda Agrobotic法国、西班牙北部和葡萄牙销售总监Jonathan Chiavassa解释道。“我们已经为番茄安装了多台多头秤，但没有为苹果自动化包装设计的产品。因此，这个解决方案在我们的包装产品中代表了真正的附加值。”

专为袋装或托盘自动化包装而设计的EW20，采用关联称重技术，自动识别最佳水果组合以达到目标重量，具有高精度。这种方法减少了过量包装，同时限制了水果的处理。

适应不断变化的包装市场趋势
引入这一新解决方案也使Maf Roda Agrobotic能够进入那些包装主要基于重量而非水果尺寸的市场细分领域。“这台机器使我们能够瞄准那些根据规定重量而非校准尺寸进行包装的市场，”Christophe Nivet解释道。“它提供了高度的灵活性，因为单个系统可以配置一个出口连接到装袋机，另一个出口连接到托盘包装线。”这种多功能性对于活跃在出口市场的运营商尤其有吸引力，因为不同目的地的展示要求各不相同。“某些市场，特别是美国（欧洲以外）、英国，甚至欧洲内部的德国，对袋装苹果有强烈偏好，而这种包装形式在其他一些欧洲国家仍不太发达。得益于这台机器，我们的客户可以拓宽产品供应，进入新市场。对于出口商来说，这是一个显著的优势。”

一项在德国已成熟的技术
尽管EW20在法国市场相对较新，但它已在莱茵河对岸享有盛誉。由总部位于汉堡附近的Strauss公司开发，该机器在德国水果包装设施中广泛使用。“这是德国包装厂中非常常见的设备，”
contentHtml: With the acquisition of the German company Strauss Verpackungsmaschinen, announced in early 2025, Maf Roda Agrobotic is continuing to integrate new technologies into its portfolio of solutions. Among these innovations, the EW20 multi-head weigher strengthens the group's offering in the apple packaging…
